title: 52" Waterfall-Framed Console
summary: A narrow entry console with the same continuous-grain waterfall miter as this catalog's coffee table — mitered 1x3 face frames top and bottom, a shelf, and inside side panels sandwiched between them.
description:
This console applies the waterfall-framed coffee table's signature 45°-mitered face frame technique to a taller, narrower entryway piece — two identical mitered 1x3 frames, one for the top and one that becomes the visible base, with the 1x12 top and sides sandwiched between them. Building both face frames first and checking that they're truly identical (stack them and compare directly) is the step that determines whether the whole piece reads as one continuous frame or two slightly different ones.

Assembly happens face-frame-first: lay one frame down with its pocket holes facing up, attach the beveled top so its outside edge is perfectly flush and its corners match the frame's corners exactly, then add the sides with the same flush-edge discipline. The bottom shelf goes in next, and only then does the whole thing get flipped over so the second face frame can be attached — building the piece as a stack rather than a box lets you check flush and square at every single interface as you go, instead of discovering a gap after the whole thing is closed up.

The inside side panels get fit last, placed inside the outer legs and pinned with brad nails plus a few pocket screws driven up through the bottom — a detail that adds real rigidity to a narrow, tall piece that would otherwise rely entirely on the face frames for its structure.

cutList.note: Long point to long point, both ends cut at 45°, ends not parallel.
Long point to long point, both ends cut at 45°, ends not parallel.
Long point to long point, both ends cut at 45° bevel, ends not parallel.
Long point measurement, one end cut at 45° bevel.

steps.title: Cut all the parts
steps.body: Cut two face frame long members at 52" and two face frame short members at 30" from the 1x3. Cut one top at 52", two sides at 30", one bottom shelf at 50-1/2" and two inside sides at 26-3/4" from the 1x12. Allowing about 1/8" for each saw cut, that comes to two 8 ft 1x3 and two 10 ft 1x12. Square every cut and label each piece as it comes off the saw.

steps.title: Build the two face frames
steps.body: Build two identical mitered face frames, carefully matching corners — stack them on top of each other and confirm they're exact matches before continuing. This is the step that determines the whole piece's final look.

steps.title: Attach the top
steps.body: Lay one face frame down with its pocket holes facing upward. Attach the beveled top so its outside edge is perfectly flush and its corners match the frame exactly. Don't overdrive the pocket hole screws.

steps.title: Attach the sides
steps.body: Drill pocket holes along both edges of the two side pieces, avoiding the area where the bottom shelf will attach. Attach the sides to the face frame, carefully matching the outside edges. Attach the 1x12 corners together with 1-1/4" brad nails and glue.

steps.title: Add the bottom shelf
steps.body: Drill 3/4" pocket holes along all four sides of the bottom shelf and attach to the face frame and sides with 1-1/4" pocket hole screws.

steps.title: Attach the second face frame
steps.body: Flip the assembly over and attach the second face frame with 1-1/4" pocket hole screws.

steps.title: Fit the inside sides and finish
steps.body: Place the inside side panels inside the legs and attach with 1-1/4" brad nails, plus a few pocket screws driven up through the bottom into the inside panels' bottom edge for extra rigidity. Sand and finish.
